meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Analytics Setup to Scale Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building blind. Master analytics setup to prove ROI to clients. A high-efficiency guide for AI builders using Antigravity and Claude Code tools.
content.answer_block: Most freelancers fail because they ship code without data. To solve this, integrate lightweight telemetry early. By coupling Antigravity for rapid instrumentation and Claude Code for automated implementation, you transform basic prototypes into data-driven assets that demonstrate real value, justify your premium rates, and provide actionable insights for your clients.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect interface, but silence greets your deployment. The client asks, 'Is it working?' and you have nothing but anecdotal feedback. You are stuck in the 'basic app' trap, where features are ghost-ships sailing into a void of zero user visibility.

It is time to stop guessing and start measuring. Professional-grade analytics aren't just for enterprise giants; they are your secret weapon for client retention. By weaving telemetry into your architecture today, you turn your code into a story of growth that keeps clients coming back for more.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that prove client value. Don't track everything; track what drives business outcomes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and suggest a schema for 3 essential user-interaction events that map directly to business value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Review project requirements and propose a JSON schema for tracking user onboarding and feature adoption events.'

steps.title: Instrument the Event Bus
steps.description: Create a centralized telemetry wrapper to avoid polluting your business logic with third-party tracking calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript event-tracking utility that abstracts the underlying provider and handles async queueing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a new utility file src/services/telemetry.ts and implement a robust event wrapper.'

steps.title: Automate Route Tracking
steps.description: Capture user navigation automatically without manual placement on every button click.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook that intercepts route changes and automatically triggers a page_view event with metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Add a custom hook usePageTracking.ts to the project and integrate it into the router provider.'

steps.title: Implement Performance Monitoring
steps.description: Latency kills conversion. Monitor your API response times to ensure the AI responses remain snappy for the end-user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an interceptor for my API client that measures request duration and logs it as a performance metric.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Update the axios instance to log performance metrics for all outgoing AI requests.'

steps.title: Build the Client Dashboard
steps.description: Visualize the data. A simple dashboard view for your client is worth more than a dozen feature updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimalist dashboard component that queries the telemetry API and displays growth trends using Recharts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Generate a new component src/components/AnalyticsDashboard.tsx and include a basic trend graph.'

pro_tips: Always add a 'debug' mode to your telemetry that logs to the console so you can verify events without waiting for the analytics pipeline.
Never send sensitive user PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to your analytics provider; hash user IDs before transmission.
Batch your events locally before sending them to the server to reduce network overhead and improve client-side performance.
Set up automated alerts for 'critical failure' events so you know the app is broken before the client even opens it.
Use custom dimensions to track which specific AI model version or prompt version generated the best user feedback.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, schedule a review meeting with your client to present the data, showing them exactly how users are interacting with your build.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to move from a 'coder' to a 'strategic partner.' Don't just ship features—ship results that you can prove. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
